children.heading: Disallowed loss or credit carried to next year
children.label: b Disallowed loss or credit carried to next year
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, any loss or credit from an activity which is disallowed under subsection (a) shall be treated as a deduction or credit allocable to such activity in the next taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: which involves the conduct of any trade or business, and

children.label: B
children.text: in which the taxpayer does not materially participate.

children.heading: Passive activity includes any rental activity
children.label: 2 Passive activity includes any rental activity
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (7), the term “passive activity” includes any rental activity.

children.heading: Working interests in oil and gas property
children.label: 3 Working interests in oil and gas property

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “passive activity” shall not include any working interest in any oil or gas property which the taxpayer holds directly or through an entity which does not limit the liability of the taxpayer with respect to such interest.

children.heading: Income in subsequent years
children.label: B Income in subsequent years
children.text: If any taxpayer has any loss for any taxable year from a working interest in any oil or gas property which is treated as a loss which is not from a passive activity, then any net income from such property (or any property the basis of which is determined in whole or in part by reference to the basis of such property) for any succeeding taxable year shall be treated as income of the taxpayer which is not from a passive activity. If the preceding sentence applies to the net income from any property for any taxable year, any credits allowable under subpart B (other than section 27) or D of part IV of subchapter A for such taxable year which are attributable to such property shall be treated as credits not from a passive activity to the extent the amount of such credits does not exceed the regular tax liability of the taxpayer for the taxable year which is allocable to such net income.

children.heading: Material participation not required for paragraphs (2) and (3)
children.label: 4 Material participation not required for paragraphs (2) and (3)
children.text: Paragraphs (2) and (3) shall be applied without regard to whether or not the taxpayer materially participates in the activity.

children.heading: Trade or business includes research and experimentation activity
children.label: 5 Trade or business includes research and experimentation activity
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(A), the term “trade or business” includes any activity involving research or experimentation (within the meaning of section 174).

children.heading: Activity in connection with trade or business or production of income
children.label: 6 Activity in connection with trade or business or production of income
A
children.text: any activity in connection with a trade or business, or

children.label: B
children.text: any activity with respect to which expenses are allowable as a deduction under section 212.

children.heading: Special rules for taxpayers in real property business
children.label: 7 Special rules for taxpayers in real property business
i
children.text: paragraph (2) shall not apply to any rental real estate activity of such taxpayer for such taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: this section shall be applied as if each interest of the taxpayer in rental real estate were a separate activity.

children.heading: Taxpayers to whom paragraph applies
children.label: B Taxpayers to whom paragraph applies
i
children.text: more than one-half of the personal services performed in trades or businesses by the taxpayer during such taxable year are performed in real property trades or businesses in which the taxpayer materially participates, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such taxpayer performs more than 750 hours of services during the taxable year in real property trades or businesses in which the taxpayer materially participates.

children.heading: Real property trade or business
children.label: C Real property trade or business
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “real property trade or business” means any real property development, redevelopment, construction, reconstruction, acquisition, conversion, rental, operation, management, leasing, or brokerage trade or business.

children.heading: Closely held C corporations
children.label: i Closely held C corporations
children.text: In the case of a closely held C corporation, the requirements of subparagraph (B) shall be treated as met for any taxable year if more than 50 percent of the gross receipts of such corporation for such taxable year are derived from real property trades or businesses in which the corporation materially participates.

children.heading: Personal services as an employee
children.label: ii Personal services as an employee
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), personal services performed as an employee shall not be treated as performed in real property trades or businesses. The preceding sentence shall not apply if such employee is a 5-percent owner (as defined in section 416(i)(1)(B)) in the employer.

children.heading: Passive activity loss and credit defined
children.label: d Passive activity loss and credit defined
A
children.text: the aggregate losses from all passive activities for the taxable year, exceed

children.label: B

children.label: ii
children.text: subpart B (other than section 27) of such part IV, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the regular tax liability of the taxpayer for the taxable year allocable to all passive activities.

children.heading: Special rules for determining income or loss from a passive activity
children.label: e Special rules for determining income or loss from a passive activity

children.heading: Certain income not treated as income from passive activity
children.label: 1 Certain income not treated as income from passive activity
I
children.text: gross income from interest, dividends, annuities, or royalties not derived in the ordinary course of a trade or business,

children.label: II
children.text: expenses (other than interest) which are clearly and directly allocable to such gross income, and

children.label: III
children.text: interest expense properly allocable to such gross income, and

children.label: I
children.text: producing income of a type described in clause (i), or

children.label: II
children.text: held for investment.

children.heading: Return on working capital
children.label: B Return on working capital
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), any income, gain, or loss which is attributable to an investment of working capital shall be treated as not derived in the ordinary course of a trade or business.

children.label: 2 Passive losses of certain closely held corporations may offset active income
i

children.label: ii
children.text: any item of gross income, expense, gain, or loss described in paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Compensation for personal services
children.label: 3 Compensation for personal services
children.text: Earned income (within the meaning of section 911(d)(2)(A)) shall not be taken into account in computing the income or loss from a passive activity for any taxable year.

children.heading: Dividends reduced by dividends received deduction
children.label: 4 Dividends reduced by dividends received deduction
children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (1) and (2), income from dividends shall be reduced by the amount of any dividends received deduction under section 243 or 245.

children.heading: Treatment of former passive activities
children.label: f Treatment of former passive activities
A
children.text: any unused deduction allocable to such activity under subsection (b) shall be offset against the income from such activity for the taxable year,

children.label: B
children.text: any unused credit allocable to such activity under subsection (b) shall be offset against the regular tax liability (computed after the application of paragraph (1)) allocable to such activity for the taxable year, and

children.label: C
children.text: any such deduction or credit remaining after the application of subparagraphs (A) and (B) shall continue to be treated as arising from a passive activity.

children.heading: Change in status of closely held C corporation or personal service corporation
children.label: 2 Change in status of closely held C corporation or personal service corporation
children.text: If a taxpayer ceases for any taxable year to be a closely held C corporation or personal service corporation, this section shall continue to apply to losses and credits to which this section applied for any preceding taxable year in the same manner as if such taxpayer continued to be a closely held C corporation or personal service corporation, whichever is applicable.

children.heading: Dispositions of entire interest in passive activity
children.label: g Dispositions of entire interest in passive activity
i

children.label: ii
children.text: any net income or gain for such taxable year from all other passive activities (determined after the application of subsection (b)),

children.heading: Subparagraph (A) not to apply to disposition involving related party
children.label: B Subparagraph (A) not to apply to disposition involving related party
children.text: If the taxpayer and the person acquiring the interest bear a relationship to each other described in section 267(b) or section 707(b)(1), then subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any loss of the taxpayer until the taxable year in which such interest is acquired (in a transaction described in subparagraph (A)) by another person who does not bear such a relationship to the tax­payer.

children.heading: Income from prior years
children.label: C Income from prior years
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations, income or gain from the activity for preceding taxable years shall be taken into account under subparagraph (A)(ii) for the taxable year to the extent necessary to prevent the avoidance of this section.

children.heading: Installment sale of entire interest
children.label: 3 Installment sale of entire interest
children.text: In the case of an installment sale of an entire interest in an activity to which section 453 applies, paragraph (1) shall apply to the portion of such losses for each taxable year which bears the same ratio to all such losses as the gain recognized on such sale during such taxable year bears to the gross profit from such sale (realized or to be realized when payment is completed).

children.heading: Interests in limited partnerships
children.label: 2 Interests in limited partnerships
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, no interest in a limited partnership as a limited partner shall be treated as an interest with respect to which a taxpayer materially participates.

children.heading: Treatment of certain retired individuals and surviving spouses
children.label: 3 Treatment of certain retired individuals and surviving spouses
children.text: A taxpayer shall be treated as materially participating in any farming activity for a taxable year if paragraph (4) or (5) of section 2032A(b) would cause the requirements of section 2032A(b)(1)(C)(ii) to be met with respect to real property used in such activity if such taxpayer had died during the taxable year.

children.heading: Certain closely held C corporations and personal service corporations
children.label: 4 Certain closely held C corporations and personal service corporations
A
children.text: 1 or more shareholders holding stock representing more than 50 percent (by value) of the outstanding stock of such corporation materially participate in such activity, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a closely held C corporation (other than a personal service corporation), the requirements of section 465(c)(7)(C) (without regard to clause (iv)) are met with respect to such activity.

children.heading: Participation by spouse
children.label: 5 Participation by spouse
children.text: In determining whether a taxpayer materially participates, the participation of the spouse of the taxpayer shall be taken into account.

children.heading: $25,000 offset for rental real estate activities
children.label: i $25,000 offset for rental real estate activities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any natural person, subsection (a) shall not apply to that portion of the passive activity loss or the deduction equivalent (within the meaning of subsection (j)(5)) of the passive activity credit for any taxable year which is attributable to all rental real estate activities with respect to which such individual actively participated in such taxable year (and if any portion of such loss or credit arose in another taxable year, in such other taxable year).

children.heading: Dollar limitation
children.label: 2 Dollar limitation
children.text: The aggregate amount to which paragraph (1) applies for any taxable year shall not exceed $25,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer, the $25,000 amount under paragraph (2) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by 50 percent of the amount by which the adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year exceeds $100,000.

children.heading: Special phase-out of rehabilitation credit
children.label: B Special phase-out of rehabilitation credit
children.text: In the case of any portion of the passive activity credit for any taxable year which is attributable to the rehabilitation credit determined under section 47, subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting “$200,000” for “$100,000”.

children.heading: Exception for low-income housing credit
children.label: C Exception for low-income housing credit
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any portion of the passive activity credit for any taxable year which is attributable to any credit determined under section 42.

children.label: iv
children.text: any passive activity loss or any loss allowable by reason of subsection (c)(7).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of taxable years of an estate ending less than 2 years after the date of the death of the decedent, this subsection shall apply to all rental real estate activities with respect to which such decedent actively participated before his death.

children.heading: Reduction for surviving spouse’s exemption
children.label: B Reduction for surviving spouse’s exemption
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the $25,000 amount under paragraph (2) shall be reduced by the amount of the exemption under paragraph (1) (without regard to paragraph (3)) allowable to the surviving spouse of the decedent for the taxable year ending with or within the taxable year of the estate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An individual shall not be treated as actively participating with respect to any interest in any rental real estate activity for any period if, at any time during such period, such interest (including any interest of the spouse of the individual) is less than 10 percent (by value) of all interests in such activity.

children.heading: No participation requirement for low-income housing or rehabilitation credit
children.label: B No participation requirement for low-income housing or rehabilitation credit
i

children.heading: Interest as a limited partner
children.label: C Interest as a limited partner
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, no interest as a limited partner in a limited partnership shall be treated as an interest with respect to which the taxpayer actively participates.

children.heading: Participation by spouse
children.label: D Participation by spouse
children.text: In determining whether a taxpayer actively participates, the participation of the spouse of the taxpayer shall be taken into account.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: j Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Closely held C corporation
children.label: 1 Closely held C corporation
children.text: The term “closely held C corporation” means any C corporation described in section 465(a)(1)(B).

children.heading: Regular tax liability
children.label: 3 Regular tax liability
children.text: The term “regular tax liability” has the meaning given such term by section 26(b).

children.heading: Allocation of passive activity loss and credit
children.label: 4 Allocation of passive activity loss and credit
children.text: The passive activity loss and the passive activity credit (and the $25,000 amount under subsection (i)) shall be allocated to activities, and within activities, on a pro rata basis in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Deduction equivalent
children.label: 5 Deduction equivalent
children.text: The deduction equivalent of credits from a passive activity for any taxable year is the amount which (if allowed as a deduction) would reduce the regular tax liability for such taxable year by an amount equal to such credits.

children.label: B
children.text: such losses shall not be allowable as a deduction for any taxable year.

children.heading: Qualified residence interest
children.label: 7 Qualified residence interest
children.text: The passive activity loss of a taxpayer shall be computed without regard to qualified residence interest (within the meaning of section 163(h)(3)).

children.heading: Rental activity
children.label: 8 Rental activity
children.text: The term “rental activity” means any activity where payments are principally for the use of tangible property.

children.heading: Election to increase basis of property by amount of disallowed credit
children.label: 9 Election to increase basis of property by amount of disallowed credit
children.text: For purposes of determining gain or loss from a disposition of any property to which subsection (g)(1) applies, the transferor may elect to increase the basis of such property immediately before the transfer by an amount equal to the portion of any unused credit allowable under this chapter which reduced the basis of such property for the taxable year in which such credit arose. If the taxpayer elects the application of this paragraph, such portion of the passive activity credit of such taxpayer shall not be allowed for any taxable year.

children.heading: Coordination with section 280A
children.label: 10 Coordination with section 280A
children.text: If a passive activity involves the use of a dwelling unit to which section 280A(c)(5) applies for any taxable year, any income, deduction, gain, or loss allocable to such use shall not be taken into account for purposes of this section for such taxable year.

children.heading: Aggregation of members of affiliated groups
children.label: 11 Aggregation of members of affiliated groups
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, all members of an affiliated group which files a consolidated return shall be treated as 1 corporation.

children.heading: Special rule for distributions by estates or trusts
children.label: 12 Special rule for distributions by estates or trusts
A
children.text: the basis of such interest immediately before such distribution shall be increased by the amount of any passive activity losses allocable to such interest, and

children.label: B
children.text: such losses shall not be allowable as a deduction for any taxable year.

children.heading: Separate application of section in case of publicly traded partnerships
children.label: k Separate application of section in case of publicly traded partnerships

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This section shall be applied separately with respect to items attributable to each publicly traded partnership (and subsection (i) shall not apply with respect to items attributable to any such partnership). The preceding sentence shall not apply to any credit determined under section 42, or any rehabilitation credit determined under section 47, attributable to a publicly traded partnership to the extent the amount of any such credits exceeds the regular tax liability attributable to income from such partnership.

children.label: A
children.text: interests in such partnership are traded on an established securities market, or

children.label: B
children.text: interests in such partnership are readily tradable on a secondary market (or the substantial equivalent thereof).

children.heading: Coordination with subsection (g)
children.label: 3 Coordination with subsection (g)
children.text: For purposes of subsection (g), a taxpayer shall not be treated as having disposed of his entire interest in an activity of a publicly traded partnership until he disposes of his entire interest in such partnership.

children.heading: Application to regulated investment companies
children.label: 4 Application to regulated investment companies
children.text: For purposes of this section, a regulated investment company (as defined in section 851) holding an interest in a qualified publicly traded partnership (as defined in section 851(h)) shall be treated as a taxpayer described in subsection (a)(2) with respect to items attributable to such interest.
